channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-6142] Enforce Linux kernel coding style in all code
item.description: With the removal of wrappers Lustre used to support multiple platforms we can now focus on enforcing the kernel coding style in the client code to minimize the difference between the Intel branch and what is upstream.
item.summary: Enforce Linux kernel coding style in all code

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

That is strange. How does sanity-lsnapshot.sh run then? Make we need to replace it with $mds1_FSTYPE" = "ldiskfs". Does that work for you Alex?

so just to clarify: if I start racer with ldiskfs, then RACER_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT=false – no snapshots, with zfs - RACER_ENABLE_SNAPSHOT= and then racer sets it to true and use snapshots.

Make we need to replace it with $mds1_FSTYPE" = "ldiskfs". Does that work for you Alex?

will test separately with ldiskfs and report back.

Tim, rather than continuing to add patches to this ticket for every issue related to upstream kernel issues, it would be better going forward to have a separate ticket like "SPDX license for all files" so that it can be tracked and completed in a reasonable time. This ticket has been open for many years, and is not really a good place to keep adding new patches.

Arshad, thanks as always for continuing your efforts in this area.

I ran a quick check (script attached, usage: git tabcheck [dir ...]) git-tabcheck and it looks like we are doing pretty well in most places, but there are still a few subsystems that need further cleanup. In particular, obdecho, ldlm, mgc, and ptlrpc are below 80%, and the test scripts also need some attention. You might consider to make a separate LU ticket for the test script cleanups so that this one can finally be retired once the code is fully converted.

Hi Andreas,

I grabbed libcfs/libcfs/debug.c to start with. Since the largest "owner" of those converted lines is Robert, I pushed the patch with the author of him. I'll look into other files under libcfs that can be added into the above patch. I'll also work on the cleanup on other modules.

Project: fs/lustre-release

Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a724bdbfea8bbeabdc08d63030b0bc087c6008c7

For the high-level current summary of source file tab vs. space line conversion (e.g. find libcfs -name "*.[ch]" | xargs grep "^\t" | wc -l):

subsystem       spaces      tabs    %done
---------       ------      ----    -----
libcfs            3332      6836      67%
lnet             22860     35069      60%
lustre           78197    183250      70%
scripts           5310     31745      85%

I'm not necessarily an advocate of mass conversion of 8-space indents to tabs, since there are a number of other style issues that could/should be addressed at the same time. If anyone is planning on doing this cleanup, I'd probably suggest grabbing one or two files to start with, convert spaces to tabs, then run it through checkpatch to find additional warnings, and visually inspect the result to see what else needs to be done (aligning continued lines, align variable and struct declarations, convert scripts to match coding style, etc). If bugs are found in the code during this inspection, the bugs fixes should be submitted in a separate patch, so that they can more easily be backported to maintenance releases.

Yu Jian, it would probably have been enough to get the total count of unconverted lines per module or similar.

For patches only converting of spaces to tabs, I was also thinking that it makes sense to see who is the largest "owner" of those converted lines, and make that person the patch Author. That way one can more easily see via "git blame" who should be asked questions about the code, if there are problems.

I’ll clean up the unchanged Lustre client codes to make them use Linux kernel coding style.

For all the code we have been changing and checking in yes, but a lot of the code has not been touched yet. For example if you drop lnetself into a git kernel tree you see many lines not tabified yet. This aims to cleanup up those remaining areas.

I think we alredy do this? checkpatch warns of all transgressions and then I usually don't allow any client code that deviates from standards.

Or was there something else missing?
